title: New American Pedestrian Safety Advocacy and Green Infrastructure Design
summary: Councilor Nave shares personal account of witnessing a new American family struggling to walk in the road carrying groceries in winter due to lack of sidewalks, framing the project as essential equity infrastructure. Council member Ehrenreich advocates for green infrastructure integration: snow swales, rain management, and tree canopy to improve year-round usability and reduce maintenance burden. Engineer confirms preliminary design will incorporate these elements. Public comment period closing Friday; public meeting was well-attended. Discussion of regional market expansion plans and proposed Burn Dairy store affecting future traffic patterns.
